Annual global oil demand growth is often thought to be about 1 million barrels/year.

Today's EIA monthly data indicates the Annual demand growth in the US alone, was just under 600,000 bbls/year.   When you add in the 3rd World, one wonders what it is?#OOTT

quoted_text:
Dont confuse inventory timing with fundamentals healing.  

Were in a much worse position now than we were in February. The buffers have been drawn down hard. If another shock hits (war escalation, major disruption, or black swan) theres very little cushion left to absorb it.  

Hell, even in the base case with no new shock, the system has already taken structural damage. Higher prices or negative second-order effects later this year and into 2027 are highly probable.   

Diplomacy and temporary

quoted_text:
Hormuz traffic holds steady

The Strait of Hormuz remained open and active on 30 June, with 34 verified crossings recorded and traffic evenly split by direction. The dataset showed a broad mix of commercial, energy-linked and support movements, while route visibility remained fragmented across Iranian, Omani, IMO and Dark or Unknown routes. That pattern points to continued operational continuity, but not a settled return to normal routing. With IMO’s incident tracker now listing 49 confirmed reg

text:
@crudechronicle Oil demand is roughly income elastic. Both the GFC and Covid resulted in parallel shifts downward in these upward-sloping curves. Remains to be seen what the residual impact of this year's drama will be, but absent these events, demand would be noticeably higher.
